title: Cast json to `datetime` didn't have a correct fsp
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
create table t (j json);
insert into t values (cast(cast("2024-10-24 11:11:11.12346" as datetime(6)) as json));
select cast(j as datetime(6)) from t;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
+----------------------------+
| cast(j as datetime(6))     |
+----------------------------+
| 2024-10-24 11:11:11.123460 |
+----------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
+------------------------+
| cast(j as datetime(6)) |
+------------------------+
| 2024-10-24 11:11:11    |
+------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

Actually, there is another bug: `insert into t values (cast("2024-10-24 11:11:11.12346" as datetime(6)));` will fail on MySQL, but it successes on TiDB.

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, j JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP(6));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, j, created_at) VALUES (1, CAST('2024-10-24 11:11:11.12346' AS JSONB), NOW());
INSERT INTO test_table (id, j, created_at) VALUES (2, CAST('2024-10-24 11:11:11.12346' AS JSONB), CAST('2024-10-24 11:11:11.12346' AS TIMESTAMP(6)));
SELECT id, CAST(j AS TEXT) AS casted_datetime FROM test_table WHERE j IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, CAST(j AS TEXT) AS casted_datetime FROM test_table WHERE created_at < NOW();
key_bug_features: Casting json to datetime loses fractional seconds
